title: Build a useFetch Hook
prompt: Build a `useFetch<T>(url: string)` hook that returns `{ data: T | null, loading: boolean, error: string | null }`. It should re-fetch whenever the URL changes and clean up in-flight requests on unmount.
context: Data fetching in hooks follows a standard pattern: set loading, trigger the fetch, update state on completion, handle errors, and cancel stale requests on cleanup. The AbortController API lets you cancel a fetch when a component unmounts or the URL changes.
